narrative: FY2022-23 leadership is split. On product-information coverage, JSW and SAIL are the clear leaders, each reporting 100% for both environmental/social product information and recycling/safe-disposal information. Tata Steel is mid-pack at 50% and 19%, while Jindal Stainless is the laggard at 0% and 4.1%. The dealer/distributor and trading-house metrics do not differentiate peers in FY2022-23 because all values are missing and therefore ranked last under the defined rule. Tata’s FY2022-23 values also require caution because the dataset flags them as consolidated, including international operations, whereas most other company-years are standalone Indian operations. Overall, SAIL and JSW lead the theme quantitatively for FY2022-23, but this appears driven by product-information coverage methodology rather than necessarily superior product circularity. Jindal’s lag is disclosure-driven, especially given stainless steel’s inherent recyclability, which becomes visible in later-year disclosures.
